name: Lemon-Date Couscous Cake
recipeIngredient: Finely grated zest of one lemon (or to taste)
2 tablespoons kudzu or arrowroot powder dissolved in 2 tablespoons apple juice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat the apple juice to boiling. Add dates and salt.
2. Cover, reduce heat and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
3. While the dates are simmering, heat a large heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-low heat. Add couscous and cook for 5-10 minutes, until lightly toasted. Stir as necessary.
4. Gradually add the toasted couscous to the simmering apple juice and dates, stirring constantly. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes, until the mixture thickens. Stir in lemon zest and remove from heat.
5. Rinse a 10-inch round or 9-inch square glass or ceramic baking pan with water. Shake out excess water, do not dry. Spread the couscous mixture evenly in the pan. Set aside to cool.
6. In a small saucepan, whisk the apple juice and maple syrup. Heat just to simmer and add the dissolved kudzu, whisking constantly. Cook briefly, whisking until thick. Spread on the cooled couscous.
Makes 9-12 servings at least.
